question: How tightly should {{target}} be integrated with {{system}}?
plain_language_preface: We're integrating {{target}} with {{system}}. Integration depth determines coupling, testability in isolation, and how hard it is to swap {{target}} later.

options.label: Event-driven — {{target}} publishes events; {{system}} subscribes
options.tradeoff: Loose coupling; debugging event chains is harder.

options.label: Direct call — {{system}} calls {{target}} interface synchronously
options.tradeoff: Simple mental model; tight coupling — swapping {{target}} requires refactor.

options.label: Adapter layer — thin wrapper translates between both surfaces
options.tradeoff: Swappable; adapter maintenance adds a new file + test surface.

options.label: Shared data — both read from a common data contract (no direct call)
options.tradeoff: Decoupled; contract schema becomes a new dependency.

options.label: Skip integration — mock {{target}} surface for now
options.tradeoff: Fastest to ship; real integration deferred to next stage.

recommended_rationale: Event-driven integration has the lowest blast radius when either side changes; use direct call (B) only for performance-critical hot paths.
